scenarios.opening_query: My restaurant depends on two chefs whose paperwork situation just got complicated.
scenarios.hidden_fact: One was picked up at a checkpoint driving home — he's in detention with a removal hearing, and the family asked me to help find counsel fast.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: One was detained at a checkpoint driving home — removal hearing coming, and his family asked me to find counsel fast.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether detention and removal proceedings are underway
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: It's renewal-stage — both work permits expire in 90 days and the petitions need filing correctly.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: It's renewals — both permits expire in 90 days and need filing right.

scenarios.opening_query: My wife's citizenship interview went wrong and now we're getting letters that scare us both.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: International Law > General Immigration/Visas || Criminal Law > Other
scenarios.hidden_fact: The letters are from a federal prosecutor — they claim her old asylum application contained false statements and are weighing criminal charges; criminal counsel comes before anything immigration.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: They're from a federal prosecutor — alleged false statements in her old asylum application, possible charges. Criminal counsel first.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether criminal exposure precedes the immigration fix
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: The letters are USCIS requests for more evidence — the N-400 just needs a proper response package.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: They're USCIS evidence requests — the application needs a proper response.

scenarios.opening_query: Our au pair's program sponsor terminated her placement over a dispute that wasn't her fault.
scenarios.final: Labor & Employment > Wage and Hour Claims - Employee
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: International Law > General Immigration/Visas || Labor & Employment > Wage and Hour Claims - Employee
scenarios.hidden_fact: The 'dispute' is that she asked for her withheld stipend — three months unpaid by the host family before us; her wage claim is the case she wants brought, and she's asked us to help.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: The 'dispute' was her asking for three months of withheld stipend from her prior host family — the wage claim is what she wants brought.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether unpaid wages underlie the program termination
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: No wage issue — her grace period is running and transferring her program status is the urgent filing.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: No wage issue — her grace period's running and the status transfer is urgent.
scenarios.vetting_note: Wage-theft fact inside a visa-program frame.

scenarios.opening_query: ICE came to my cousin's workplace and now the whole family is preparing for the worst.
scenarios.hidden_fact: They weren't there for him — it was an I-9 audit of the employer; he has DACA, and what he actually needs is his renewal filed early and correctly.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: They came for the employer's I-9 audit, not him — he has DACA and needs his renewal filed early and right.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether enforcement targeted the individual or the employer
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: They detained him at the site — he's in the county contract facility awaiting a bond hearing.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: They detained him at the site — he's awaiting a bond hearing.
scenarios.vetting_note: Audit-vs-arrest fact behind identical family panic.

scenarios.opening_query: My fiancé arrived on his visa but the wedding got postponed by a family death, and now we're past a deadline.
scenarios.hidden_fact: The K-1 90-day window passed — he's now out of status, and a missed-deadline overstay case with removal exposure needs handling, not just a late filing.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: The 90-day K-1 window passed — he's out of status with removal exposure, not just a late form.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the missed window created removal exposure
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: We married on day 89 — the deadline we missed is just the adjustment paperwork, which can be filed now.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: We married on day 89 — the missed deadline is just the adjustment paperwork.

scenarios.opening_query: My parents' green cards were approved years ago but a border officer just flagged something on their reentry.
scenarios.hidden_fact: They'd been abroad 14 months caring for my grandmother — the officer says they abandoned residency and issued NTAs; it's removal defense on abandonment grounds now.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: They were abroad 14 months for my grandmother — the officer called it abandonment and issued NTAs. It's removal defense now.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether abandonment allegations and NTAs issued
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: The flag was a name mix-up resolved at secondary — what they want now is naturalization so this never happens again.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: It was a name mix-up, resolved at secondary — now they want naturalization so it never recurs.
